name: Template Water Heater
description: The `template` water heater platform allows you to create simple water heaters out of just a few actions and lambdas. Once defined, it will automatically appear in Home Assistant as a water heater entity and can be controlled through the frontend.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name for the water heater. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the water heater in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Restore Mode
config_entries.description: Control how the water heater attempts to restore state on bootup.

config_entries.label: Away
config_entries.description: Lambda to be evaluated repeatedly to get the current away mode state. Expects a boolean return value.

config_entries.label: Current Temperature
config_entries.description: Lambda to be evaluated repeatedly to get the current temperature of the water. Expects a float return value.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Is On
config_entries.description: Lambda to be evaluated repeatedly to get the current On/Off state. Expects a boolean return value.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Lambda to be evaluated repeatedly to get the current operation mode. Expects a `WaterHeaterMode` enum return value.

config_entries.label: Optimistic
config_entries.description: Whether to operate in optimistic mode - when in this mode, any command sent to the template water heater will immediately update the reported state. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Set Action
config_entries.description: The action to perform when the water heater receives a command (mode change, target temperature change, away, on/off, etc.). This is where you implement the actual control logic for your water heater.

config_entries.label: Supported Modes
config_entries.description: Static list of operation modes that will be exposed to the frontend (for example Home Assistant). This controls the `operation_list` reported to Home Assistant and affects only the UI and available service calls. It does not change runtime behavior or control logic. When not specified, all supported water heater modes are shown by default. > [!NOTE] > The list of `supported_modes` is static and evaluated at startup. It cannot be changed dynamically and does not support templates or lambdas.

config_entries.label: Target Temperature
config_entries.description: Lambda to be evaluated repeatedly to get the target temperature of the water. Expects a float return value.

config_entries.label: Max Temperature
config_entries.description: Override the maximum temperature shown in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Min Temperature
config_entries.description: Override the minimum temperature shown in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Target Temperature Step
config_entries.description: Override the temperature steps shown in the frontend.
